claimText: The target for achieving 20% ethanol blending in petrol (E20) was advanced to 2025-26 under the National Policy on Biofuels.
evidenceExcerpt: Target of 20% ethanol blending in petrol was advanced to 2025-26 from 2030 under the National Policy on Biofuels.
examUse: Statement-based question on the targets of the National Policy on Biofuels.

claimText: Ethanol is a high-octane fuel but is 27% less energy-dense than petrol.
evidenceExcerpt: Ethanol is 27% less energy-dense than petrol but has a high-octane rating.
examUse: Comparison question on the physical and chemical properties of ethanol versus conventional petrol.
distractorHooks: Ethanol is more energy-dense than petrol
Ethanol has a lower octane rating than petrol

claimText: India is the world's third-largest producer and consumer of ethanol, ranking behind the USA and Brazil.
evidenceExcerpt: India is the world's third-largest producer and consumer of ethanol, after the USA and Brazil.
examUse: Fact-based statement in a multi-statement question regarding India's global standing in biofuel production.
distractorHooks: India is the largest producer of ethanol in the world
India is the second-largest producer of ethanol

claimText: Distillers' dried grains (DDGS) are a by-product of grain ethanol production that are reused as animal feed.
evidenceExcerpt: Distillers' dried grains (DDGS) are a by-product of grain ethanol reused as animal feed.
examUse: Correct-pair or application-based question on the by-products of biofuel production and their economic utility.
distractorHooks: DDGS is a toxic waste product requiring specialized disposal
DDGS is a by-product of biodiesel production from used cooking oil

claimText: The PM JIVAN Yojana is specifically designed to facilitate and support Second Generation (2G) ethanol projects.
evidenceExcerpt: PM JIVAN Yojana facilitates Second Generation (2G) ethanol projects.
examUse: Scheme-objective matching or statement-based question on government initiatives for advanced biofuels.
distractorHooks: PM JIVAN Yojana supports First Generation (1G) ethanol projects
PM JIVAN Yojana is aimed at promoting solar energy infrastructure

claimText: The GST rate on ethanol meant for the Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme has been reduced to 5%.
evidenceExcerpt: GST on ethanol meant for the EBP Programme was reduced from 18% to 5%.
examUse: Statement-based question on government fiscal incentives to promote the Ethanol Blended Petrol Programme.
distractorHooks: Ethanol for the EBP Programme is completely exempted from GST
The GST on ethanol for the EBP Programme is 18%
